free_text: Inelastic interaction of 14-MeV neutrons
with nuclei
Eng.transl.of AE,2,68,1957
Data are given
Absolute measurements
Shell transmission
Threshold detectors (Cu-63(n,2n)Cu-62),
To estimate energy disribution of the scattered
neutrons, Cu63(n,2n)Cu62 and Al27(n,p)Mg27 with
thresholds 3. and 12.5 MeV, respectively, were used.
Electrostatic accelerating tube,
deuteron energy 110 keV
Tritium-zirconium target , 14 mm diameter.
Cross section was defined as:
= -1/(n*d) * ln(b/a), where
n- number of nuclei in 1cm3 of scatterer,
d - effective thickness of the scatterer, cm,
b and a - activities of detectors irradiated with and
without scatterer.
Multiple scattering was not taken into account.
Type of error is not explained in article
On neutron flux attenuation in the sample ,
on neutron path variation in the scatterer and
in the detector, on neutron beam divergency
Tbl. from SJA,2,72,1957
Compiled in CJD
Institute and Status codes corrected
SD: Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Ref. on Engl.transl. of AE added. STATUS moved to
Subent 1. SAMPLE added to Subents 2-20.
M.M. Article was checked, corrections were
done in DECAY-DATA,INC-SOURCE,SAMPLE .
ANALYSIS was added. DATA and DATA-ERR values were
corrected as given in Table 2.
The scatterers, were chiseled or cast. The thickness
of the scattering shell was not greater than 2 cm.

The iodine was pressed from fine crystals and powders
and was placed in thin-walled containers. When was
irradiated, the detector, after having been irradiated
without the scatterer, was placed in the same container
as the scatterer.
The thickness of the scattering shell was not
greater than 2 cm.
When was irradiated, the detector, after having been
irradiated without the scatterer, was placed in the
same container as the scatterer. The thickness of the
scattering shell was not greater than 2 cm.
The tungsten was pressed from fine crystals and powders
and was placed in thin-walled containers. When was
irradiated, the detector, after having been irradiated
without the scatterer, was placed in the same container
as the scatterer.
The thickness of the scattering shell was not greater
than 2 cm.
When was irradiated, the detector, after having been
irradiated without the scatterer, was placed in the
same container as the scatterer.
In all cases, the thickness of the scattering shell
was not greater than 2 cm. Multiple scattering was not
taken into account.
The scatterers, were chiseled or cast. The thickness
of the scattering shell was not greater than 2 cm.
